description: One-stop entry point for the NeonCart AI Observability demo.

options.content: NeonCart support copilot  →  AI observability with Sigil

Every Claude call. Every tool. Every Ollama sub-agent. Traced.

NeonCart’s support copilot delegates to five small specialists. They hallucinate, they leak language, they violate schemas. Sigil catches it — in traces, evaluators, cost, and conversation quality. Click any preview to drill in.

1

No black-box AI

Multi-step trace tree, one click deep.

Every customer chat is a parent chat span with N assistant_turn children, each with their tool calls and any Ollama sub-agent hand-offs nested below. Walk a slow turn, find the specialist that hallucinated, see the exact prompt and response — no guessing, no log-grepping, no tribal knowledge.

What you see in the trace
“Twelve calls into one trace, and we found the bad refund draft came from the policy-drafter specialist on its third retry.”
Open dashboard →

2

Catch the bad output before customers do

LLM-judge + schema + regex evaluators on every generation.

Eight evaluators run in-line: hallucination check, JSON schema, PII regex, language match, groundedness, faithfulness, prompt-injection, tone. Failures are tagged by category — fabricated product, ungrounded policy claim, uncited specifics — and roll up to a per-evaluator pass-rate panel.

Live failure mix
“Three categories of fail in the last hour: ungrounded_policy_claim, claimed_action_not_taken, uncited_specifics.”
Open dashboard →

3

See the whole agent tree

Claude → 5 Ollama specialists, parent/child generation chains.

When the support copilot delegates — translate, summarize, draft a refund, extract metadata, recommend a product — Sigil records it as a child generation with a parent_generation_id. The visualizer draws the tree, latency-by-hop, specialist error rate, and per-model token spend.

Sub-agent insight
“TinyLlama summarizer averages 31% groundedness fails. We saw it without writing a query.”
Open dashboard →

4

Treat AI cost like infra cost

P50/P95 latency, TTFT, token spend, by model + agent.

Auto-emitted from the OTel GenAI semantic conventions: gen_ai_client_time_to_first_token_seconds, gen_ai_client_operation_duration_seconds, gen_ai_client_token_usage_count. P95 TTFT for streaming. Token mix per model. The spend panel pivots by use case.

From the latency dashboard
“Streaming TTFT P95 is 1.2s on Sonnet, 3.6s on Opus. Cache savings = 41% of input tokens last hour.”
Open dashboard →

More views

Nine more focused dashboards

Latency · errors · retries
Tool execution →

KB hit rate · relevance · faithfulness
RAG quality →

Versions · model routing · agent fleet
Prompt insights →

Cards · SSNs · API keys
PII / guardrails →

Fabrications · ungrounded claims
Hallucination & groundedness →

$ / model · cache savings · top spenders
Cost & token spend →

Tool errors · max_tokens · refusals
Errors & stop reasons →

Escalations · turn count · frustration
Conversation quality →

Refunds · KB-deflection · resolution rate
Business KPIs →

How to drive the demo

Open the NeonCart storefront chat. The seed prompts include failure buttons (PII paste, prompt injection, German/Japanese, hallucination bait). Send a few — they route through the agent and delegate to small Ollama specialists that intentionally produce flawed output. Within 30s the cards above will populate.

Stack

Stack: stephenwagner.grafana.net
App: NeonCart support copilot
Sigil SDK: Python · auto-emits OTel GenAI conventions
